Add issue types to issue templates (#6139)

Adds the `type` field to each issue template so newly created issues are
automatically assigned the correct issue type.

| Template | Issue Type |
|---|---|
| Bug_Report.yml | Bug |
| Documentation_Issue.yml | Task |
| Feature_Request.yml | Feature |

CodeFlow](https://microsoft.github.io/open-pr/?codeflow=https://github.com/microsoft/winget-cli/pull/6139)

commit.message:
fix(ui): defer SplitterDistance to OnShown to avoid layout crash

Setting SplitterDistance during object initialisation throws
'SplitterDistance must be between Panel1MinSize and Width - Panel2MinSize'
because the form has zero width at construction time.

Fix:
- Promote SplitContainer to a field (_split)
- Remove SplitterDistance from the initialiser
- Set _split.SplitterDistance = Width / 2 in OnShown, after the
  form has been laid out and has a real pixel width

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Compact pipe-delimited table format for keypoint time series

~50% token reduction vs JSON (joint names appear once in header,
not repeated per frame; no JSON syntax overhead).

Format example:
  t | nose | l_sho | r_sho | l_elb | r_elb | l_wri | r_wri | ...
  .00 | - | .41,.52 | .43,.51 | - | - | .38,.81 | .44,.80 | ...
  1.67 | - | .41,.59 | .43,.58 | - | - | .38,.88 | .44,.87 | ...

Changes:
- Drop eyes/ears (13 joints instead of 17 — never needed for rep counting)
- Abbreviated joint names: left_shoulder -> l_sho, right_wrist -> r_wri etc.
- Strip leading zeros: 0.41 -> .41 (saves 1 token per value)
- '-' for undetected joints (vs omitting from JSON object)
- _JOINT_SHORT / _JOINT_ORDER / _TABLE_HEADER defined at module level
- _build_keypoint_table() replaces _build_keypoint_series()
- Remove json import (no longer needed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add tarpaulin coverage job (#23)

* feat: implement Phase 3 e-commerce heuristics and related product pruning

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe
     `Result` propagation.
   - Refactored `RipwebError` and domain errors (`DdgError`, `GithubError`, etc.)
     to leverage the `thiserror` crate.
   - Updated `main()` to return `anyhow::Result<()>`, migrating `std::process::exit()`
     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

Also updates `CURRENT_PRIORITIES.md` to mark these milestones as completed.

* fix(ci): repair PR #7 branch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(mode): add 7-variant Mode enum per output contract

* feat(cli): replace --verbosity u8 with --mode Mode

* fix(cli): update verbosity_default test to use Mode::Balanced

* feat(run): thread Mode through format functions, add source delimiters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace verbosity: u8 with mode: crate::mode::Mode in all 6 search
platform files, dispatching via mode.density_tier(). Update inline
tests to pass Mode variants instead of raw integers.

* fix(normalize): strip UTM and tracking params from cache keys per output contract

* feat(output): emit mode/token/results header per output contract

* test(output_contract): rewrite tests against Mode enum, add delimiter/tracking coverage

Also fixes apostle_extraction.rs which had the same u8 verbosity → Mode type errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(ui): replace TreeView with dual-pane ListView file manager

Full rewrite of MainForm as a side-by-side file manager (Explorer-style):

Local pane (left):
- KryptonListView in Details mode: Name, Size, Modified, Type columns
- Browse button + path bar + Up button for navigation
- '..' entry at top to go up one directory level
- Double-click folder to enter; files shown with size/date

S3 pane (right):
- Same layout: Name, Size, Modified, Key columns
- Auto-loads bucket root on form shown
- Prefix-based virtual navigation (double-click prefix to enter)

Toolbar: Upload, Download, Sync (with preview), Refresh buttons
- Upload: uploads selected local files to current S3 prefix
- Download: downloads selected S3 objects to current local path
- Sync: opens SyncPreviewForm then executes confirmed plan

Status bar shows current operation / transfer progress.
ConflictResolutionDialog defined at bottom of file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
LLM counts reps from keypoint time series; segments schema

Core insight: keypoints at 10fps are a better rep-counting signal than
images at 1fps. The LLM receives a structured time series of joint
positions and counts reps by analyzing motion patterns directly.

vlm_coach.py:
- Revert multi-image buffer -> single grounding image (_latest_snapshot)
- Add _kp_buffer: deque of (ts, keypoints) tuples, 60 frames (~6s at 10fps)
- _build_keypoint_series(): subsample to 15 evenly-spaced frames (~3fps)
  with relative timestamps, compact JSON format
- Schema: CoachingResult now contains segments: list[ExerciseSegment]
  ExerciseSegment: {exercise, reps, form_score, tip}
- System prompt explains rep-counting from joint motion patterns
  (shoulder Y osc = push-up, hip Y osc = squat, wrist spread = jumping_jack)
- maybe_call() no longer takes pending_reps (LLM does its own counting)
- Image is optional grounding; keypoint time series is the primary signal

rep_counter.py:
- confirm_window() now takes list[ExerciseSegment] instead of str
- Uses LLM's reps directly instead of distributing pending_reps
- Aligns motion hint to last segment's exercise after confirmation
- pending_reps still used for live display estimate between LLM calls

server.py:
- vlm_scheduler passes result.segments to confirm_window()
- Logs all segment (exercise, reps) pairs per LLM call
- Tips collected from all segments, not just first
- process_frame derives form_score/tip from last segment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent impl-review and plan-review from silently producing zero output on large PRs (#117)

* Initial plan

* fix: improve impl-review and plan-review workflow reliability on large PRs

- Add explicit 'zero output is not acceptable' rule to both workflows
- Add noop fallback path when agent cannot read any PR content
- Add Reading Strategy section to impl-review to guide the agent to
  prioritize files and submit the review as soon as a confident decision
  can be made, rather than exhausting context on every file
- Log the root cause and fix in docs/issues/

Fixes #116

commit.message:
v1.21.16: reject untrusted domains in SearXNG Strategy 2 EAN lookup

EAN numbers can appear in trademark, patent, and legal database pages.
SearXNG's Strategy 2 was blindly using those pages' titles as product names,
producing results like 'GODLY GOAL-GETTER Trademark Application' while the
Kesko CDN correctly returned the real product image.

Fix: Strategy 2 now only uses titles from a trusted-domain whitelist
(k-ruoka.fi, s-kaupat.fi, foodie.fi, barcodelookup.com, openfoodfacts.org,
etc.). Untrusted domains fall through to Strategy 3 which returns
'Unknown product (EAN)' with the correct image.

Also synced grocy_scraper_addon copy of searxng_client.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(security): remove .env from git tracking

The .env file was committed before the .gitignore rule was added.
It only contains VITE_API_URL (public frontend config), but tracked
.env files are a security anti-pattern. The .gitignore already has
.env listed, so it won't be re-added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
flowey: add path filters to GhPrTriggers/GhCiTriggers and AdoPrTriggers/AdoCiTriggers; scope docs pipeline to Guide/** (#3020)

The docs pipeline was running on every PR/push to `main`, regardless of
whether any documentation was changed. This adds path-filter support to
flowey's GitHub and ADO trigger infrastructure and uses it to scope the
docs pipeline to only run when relevant files are modified.

## Path filters

The docs pipeline now only triggers when changes are made to:
- `Guide/**` — the documentation source
- `petri/logview/**` — the test results viewer deployed to
openvmm.dev/test-results/
- `.github/workflows/openvmm-docs-*.yaml` — the generated workflow
definitions
- `flowey/flowey_hvlite/src/pipelines/build_docs.rs` — the pipeline
source of truth

## Changes

- **`flowey_core/src/pipeline.rs`** — Added `paths` and `paths_ignore`
fields to `GhPrTriggers` and `GhCiTriggers`; added `paths` and
`exclude_paths` fields to `AdoPrTriggers` and `AdoCiTriggers`

- **`github_yaml_defs.rs`** — Added `paths` / `paths_ignore` fields
(skipped when empty) to `PrTrigger` and `CiTrigger`

- **`github_yaml/mod.rs`** — Propagate the new fields during GitHub YAML
generation

- **`schema_ado_yaml/src/lib.rs`** — Added `TriggerPaths` struct; added
optional `paths` field to `PrTrigger::Some` and `CiTrigger::Some`
variants

- **`ado_yaml.rs`** — Propagate the new path filter fields during ADO
YAML generation

- **`build_docs.rs`** — Set path filters on both PR and CI triggers

- **Generated YAMLs** — `openvmm-docs-pr.yaml` and
`openvmm-docs-ci.yaml` regenerated via `cargo xtask fmt --fix`

> **Note:** Path-filtered workflows that never run may fail GitHub
required status checks. If the `openvmm build docs gates` job is a
required check, the repo branch protection rules may need adjustment
(e.g., marking it non-required or using a skip-detection workflow).

Fixes microsoft/openvmm#3019

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: benhillis <17727402+benhillis@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Ben Hillis <benhill@ntdev.microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenVMM Team <openvmm@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
artifacts: preserve best checkpoint and held-out test eval

Preserve the actual best validation checkpoint copied from Aorus:
- artifacts/models/best_model_baseline_s789.pt
- SHA256: 76907b9ea0170636dcb09a0645518ca26c77e8640f64ec2092a03d6babf110bc

Also save supporting result artifacts from Aorus:
- retrain_v4.json
- ensemble_v4.json
- error_analysis.json
- adaptive_nms.json
- push_v5.json
- p64_result.json

Add held-out test evaluation for the preserved checkpoint:
- val F1 = 0.9792, rate MAE = 0.198 bpm
- val_adv F1 = 0.9793, rate MAE = 0.183 bpm
- test F1 = 0.9110, rate MAE = 0.515 bpm

This confirms the checkpoint reproduces the strong validation score, but the
held-out patient test split is materially lower, so 0.9792 should not be used
as the generalization estimate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set ENV PORT to match EXPOSE in web/user-dashboard/game-arena Dockerfiles

Railway routes traffic to the EXPOSE port. These services had EXPOSE set
to their default ports (3000/6001/8080) but Railway was injecting PORT=3001,
causing the app to listen on 3001 while Railway routed to the EXPOSE port.
Set ENV PORT to match EXPOSE so Railway injects the correct routing port.

Fixes 502 for web, user-dashboard, and game-arena on Railway.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: clean up Prometheus metrics on backup deletion

Add CleanupBackupMetrics and CleanupRepositoryMetrics helpers to remove
stale metric label sets when resources are deleted. Call cleanup from
the KopiaBackup finalizer to prevent unbounded cardinality growth.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add lightspeed commands to skip update check for fast exit (#7629)

* feat: add lightspeed commands to skip update check for fast exit

Extensions using AzureDeveloperCLICredential spawn 'azd auth token' as a
subprocess with a 10-second timeout. The background update check goroutine
(60s timeout) was blocking process exit in main.go, causing 'signal: killed'
errors when the update check was slow (e.g., after laptop wake, DNS stalls).

This change adds a Lightspeed property to ActionDescriptorOptions that marks
commands requiring fast exit. The update check goroutine is now started inside
ExecuteWithAutoInstall after command identification — lightspeed commands skip
it entirely, so the process exits immediately after producing output.

Changes:
- Add Lightspeed bool field to ActionDescriptorOptions
- Propagate lightspeed to cobra annotations in CobraBuilder
- Move fetchLatestVersion from main.go into startUpdateCheck() in auto_install.go
- Start update check only after rootCmd.Find(), skip for lightspeed commands
- Return ExecuteResult from ExecuteWithAutoInstall with LatestVersion channel
- Mark 'auth token' as Lightspeed: true

Fixes #7360

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add lightspeed to cspell dictionary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Restore parse-error log for invalid AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK values
- Accept context parameter in startUpdateCheck for tracing/cancellation
- Set AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK in test to avoid real HTTP calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct LatestVersion doc comment to match behavior

The channel is nil only for lightspeed commands (goroutine never starts).
When AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK skips the check, the channel is closed without
a value rather than being nil.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid real credential stack in lightspeed detection test

Use --output unknown to fail fast before touching the credential/provider
stack. The test only needs to verify IsLightspeed detection, not actual
token acquisition.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: assert LatestVersion nil/non-nil in lightspeed test

Directly test the core behavioral change: lightspeed commands must have
LatestVersion == nil (no goroutine started), non-lightspeed commands must
have LatestVersion != nil (goroutine started, even when AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK
short-circuits it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract lightspeed annotation key to shared constant

Define AnnotationLightspeed = "azd.lightspeed" in cmd/actions package
and reference it from cobra_builder.go, auto_install.go, and tests.
Uses azd. namespace prefix to avoid collisions with extension annotations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add $$$$-credential decryption to export parser

Decodes AVM's $$$$-encoded credentials using two-stage AES-256-CBC:
- Bootstrap key from password or device serial+maca
- Export key from Password= header
- AVM custom Base32 (A-Z, 1-6 alphabet)
- MD5 integrity verification per value

New CLI flags: --serial, --maca, --decode-secrets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add export patcher for firmware version + CRC32 fix

Patches FirmwareVersion= header and recalculates AVM's structured
CRC32 checksum. Useful for cross-version export imports.

CRC algorithm based on PeterPawn's decoder (GPL-2.0-or-later):
- Header: key + value + null per field
- Section paths: path + null
- CFGFILE body: lines with double-backslash collapse, skip last line
- B64FILE/BINFILE: base64/hex decoded binary per line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document why Secrets RBAC is cluster-wide

Explain that kubebuilder RBAC markers can't restrict to dynamically-named
secrets, so full CRUD is granted with controller logic limiting access.

commit.message:
fix: make auto-created backup detection resilient to status update failures

Use owner reference (via metav1.IsControlledBy) as fallback to detect
auto-created KopiaBackup objects when Status.AutoCreated may not have
been persisted (e.g., status update failed after initial Create).

Applies to both the deletion path (label removal) and the sync path
(schedule updates), ensuring the controller correctly manages backups
even after transient API errors.

pass.

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate status updates into single deferred call

Replace scattered Status().Update() calls throughout reconcile methods
with a single deferred call in the main reconcile function. Sub-methods
now only set conditions in-memory; the defer persists them atomically.
Applies to both KopiaBackup and KopiaRepository controllers.

This eliminates data loss from mid-reconcile status update conflicts and
reduces API server calls per reconciliation cycle.

feat: add existing-repo analysis protocol to githubKit Discover phase (#110)

* chore: log #17 implementation to bender history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(security): add path/ref validation and rate-limit guards (#17)

Issue 1 — Input validation:
- New github-input-validation.ts module with validatePath() and validateRef()
- Rejects path traversal (../), absolute paths, null bytes, control chars
- Validates git refs against git-check-ref-format rules (SHA, branch, tag)
- Wired into github_repo_tree and github_repo_file_read tools

Issue 2 — Rate-limit guard:
- New github-rate-limit.ts singleton tracker reads X-RateLimit-* headers
- GitHubConnector.request() overridden to enforce pre-flight check and
  update tracker from every response
- Tools surface rateLimitWarning in responses when remaining is low
- Blocks requests (RATE_LIMITED error) when quota is exhausted

Includes 32 new tests covering all validation rules and rate-limit states.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Reflect latest architectural patterns and features:
- ServicePack pattern with declarative auth and lifecycle hooks
- Fat A2UI components (Azure/GitHub login, resource pickers) with security controls

- CORS proxy security measures (IP filtering, redirect validation)

Addresses issue #52 acceptance criteria:
- README reflects current architecture and features
- API reference docs present (docs/api-reference.md)
- Component catalog documented with fat components
- Deployment guide current (docs/deployment.md)

feat(auth): add API token support for programmatic REST API access

- Add ApiToken model (SHA-256 hashed, prefix for display)
- Create /api/tokens CRUD (session-only — tokens can't manage tokens)
- Add apiAuth() helper supporting both Bearer tokens and session cookies
- Update all protected routes to accept Bearer token auth
- Middleware passes API requests with Authorization header to inline auth
- Add ApiTokenManager UI component (key icon in dashboard header)
- Update Swagger spec with bearerAuth security scheme
- Update README with authentication documentation
- lastUsedAt throttled to 5-min intervals to reduce DB writes

commit.message:
feat(direct): implement direct connection mode (no relay) (#19)

* feat(direct): implement direct connection mode (no relay)

Add direct connection mode allowing Yandex to call MA server directly
via HTTPS without the yaha-cloud.ru WebSocket relay.

New files:
- provider/direct.py: DirectConnectionHandler with HTTP routes for
  Smart Home API endpoints and OAuth account linking
- tests/test_direct.py: 36 tests covering auth, health, API, OAuth,
  and plugin integration

Modified:
- provider/plugin.py: Add _start_direct_mode() and cleanup in unload()
- provider/__init__.py: Add direct mode config entries with endpoint
  URLs, OAuth URLs, skill_id/skill_token fields
- provider/constants.py: Add direct-mode constants (paths, OAuth, etc.)
- README.md, CLAUDE.md: Document direct connection mode

The handler registers dynamic routes on MA's built-in webserver using
mass.webserver.register_dynamic_route(). OAuth implements minimal
Authorization Code flow for Yandex account linking.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(direct): address Copilot review — XSS, OAuth validation, URL normalization

- HTML-escape redirect_url in OAuth authorize page (XSS fix)
- Validate client_id, response_type, redirect_uri domain in /auth/authorize
- Validate client_id and client_secret in /auth/token endpoint
- Replace hardcoded DIRECT_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET with per-install generated secret
- Add CONF_DIRECT_CLIENT_SECRET to config entries with auto-generation
- Normalize base_url with rstrip('/') to prevent double slashes
- Preserve CONF_DIRECT_ACCESS_TOKEN value on config save
- Restructure README install steps (Cloud vs Direct sections)
- Add 5 new tests for validation (41 direct tests, 157 total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Fix CrateMess black screen caused by decompilation bug in OpenAndWait/CloseAndWait

The delegate in OpenAndWait and CloseAndWait was setting a throwaway
variable 'flag' instead of the loop condition (isDoneOpening/isDoneClosing),
causing an infinite loop. This meant CrateMess would hang forever with the
FadeView open after selecting a level, showing only a black screen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.20.25

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

VLM: send 5-frame rolling window instead of single snapshot

Previously: 1 jpeg per call (latest only)
Now: up to 5 evenly-spaced frames from a 12-frame ring buffer (~6s)

Why: a single frame gives no temporal context. If the user switches
exercises at second 1 of a 5s window, the VLM still fires at second 5
but now sees the full motion arc and can classify the current state
accurately even mid-transition.

Changes:
- _snapshot_buffer: deque(maxlen=12) of (ts, bytes) tuples
- _select_snapshots(): picks SNAPSHOT_SEND_COUNT evenly-spaced frames,
  always including the most recent
- _call_vlm(): sends all selected images first (oldest→newest),
  then the text prompt with keypoints
- System prompt updated to describe multi-frame input and ask model
  to classify based on MOST RECENT frame (not average over sequence)
- USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE includes frame_count for clarity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: support B3 UMDF SBE schema compatibility

Fix multiple code generation issues preventing the B3 UMDF market data
SBE schema (v2.2.0) from compiling:

1. DTD processing: Change DtdProcessing.Prohibit to DtdProcessing.Ignore

2. Constant type field resolution: When a message field references a
   constant type (e.g., SeqNum1 -> uint32 with value 1), resolve to the
   underlying primitive type and value instead of generating invalid code.
   Added ConstantTypeInfo dictionary to SchemaContext.

3. Named optional types in message fields: When a field uses a named
   optional type (e.g., RptSeq -> uint32 with nullValue=0), use the
   underlying primitive type directly instead of the wrapper struct.
   Propagate the null value from the type definition.

4. Composite optional fields: When a composite type (e.g., PriceOptional,
   Fixed8) is used as an optional field, fall back to a regular field
   since composites handle their own null semantics internally. This
   prevents invalid == default comparisons on structs.

5. Regular named types as optional fields: Added TypePrimitiveMapping to
   SchemaContext to track named types (e.g., SettlType -> ushort) so they
   can be properly resolved when used as optional fields.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
